 It really was about launching a company and a channel where we felt we cou=
ld get the most direct connection with our customer and build our brand
organically, knowing that wholesale was eventually a really important part =
of our strategy.

When we started strategizing what a larger wholesale expansion would look l=
ike for us, about three years ago, we were really targeting the 20 percent =
number. That number felt like we would be distributed to the extent where w=
e were acquiring customers and having brand awareness, but also where we co=
uld still really choose and have meaningful effective partnerships.

What are the benefits of partnering with Nordstrom for a brand like D=C3=B4=
en?=C2=A0

Holly Soroca: We=E2=80=99re always looking to be in the best of the best re=
tailers in each location, and we=E2=80=99ve spent a lot of time thinking ab=

n a way that is mutually beneficial. Nordstrom has locations where there ar=
e no other locations, and so we don=E2=80=99t have
any physical distribution in a lot of those places. They are the fashion au=
thority [and] have such an expertise in curating an assortment that is very=
 specific to their customer. It=E2=80=99ll look different than it=E2=80=99l=
l look in our own store and on our website.

There=E2=80=99s places like Seattle or even North Park in Dallas, Tampa, Ki=

 We sometimes get messages like, When are you opening a store here? We

=80=99s a huge amount of opportunity for brand awareness. The foot traffic =
that [Nordstrom] has is obviously very different than what we would get in =
our own boutique stores, even if we did have a store in the same location.

le=3D"margin-top:0;margin-bottom:0;list-style-type:revert;"><li style=3D"Ma=
rgin:0 0 24px 0;"><em>The
Gucci guessing game</em>: You have to hand it to Gucci. This past week, mer=
e <em>days </em>after <strong>Sabato De Sarno</strong>=E2=80=99s exit was a=
nnounced, the brand managed to stage an inoffensive runway show, dress what=
 felt like dozens of A-listers for the Oscars and afterparties, and <em>als=
o</em> sponsor the <strong>Guy Oseary</strong> party, where brand ambassado=
r <strong>Julia Garner</strong> was photographed hanging with co-host <stro=
ng>Madonna</strong>, whom she is slated to play in
an upcoming biopic. Pretty much every winner showed up; <strong>Lil Wayne</=
strong> performed. All this was happening as speculation intensified in Eur=
ope around who might be the next Gucci designer.<br/><br/>Interestingly, ve=
ry, <em>very</em> few people know what=E2=80=99s happening, despite the fac=
t that the announcement is expected to arrive soon=E2=80=94maybe even by th=
e end of Paris Fashion Week. I was told by one trusted industry source to b=
uy Kering stock because it=E2=80=99s going to be a sure-bet designer=E2=80=
=94such as
<strong>Hedi Slimane</strong>=E2=80=94but others keep pointing to lesser-kn=
own, yet still formidable candidates. <strong>Dario Vitale</strong> keeps c=
oming up, despite his conversations with Versace. One thing to remember is =
that no Gucci designer has ever been a <em>name</em> before they started at=
 Gucci, so a known entity would be a departure from that. Anyway, as my par=
tner <strong>Bill Cohan </strong>likes to say, this is not investment advic=
e.<br/><br/>Whatever happens, it=E2=80=99s clear that C.E.O.
<strong>Stefano Cantino</strong> and Kering deputy C.E.O. <strong>Francesca=
 Bellettini </strong>began their partnership with a plan, and they=E2=80=99=
ve executed this transition without <em>any </em>of the leaks that have pla=
gued the rest of the industry. To wit: As loyal readers know, I=E2=80=99ve =
been writing about <strong>Jonathan Anderson</strong>=E2=80=99s move to Dio=
r for months, and yet LVMH has still not announced it. The Gucci tandem mov=
ed off De Sarno quickly, before further damage could be wrought, and
they=E2=80=99ve kept his presumptive settlement negotiations quiet, too. Ap=
parently, they=E2=80=99ve decided that they are going to do everything in t=
heir power to make this as painless and straight-shooting as possible for t=
he markets, employees, and fashion industry observers alike.</li><li style=
=3D"Margin:0 0 24px 0;"><em>More on the LVMH machinations</em>: I got <em>q=
uite</em> a bit of feedback on yesterday=E2=80=99s intel regarding changes =
at LVMH. The consensus was that Loro Piana C.E.O. <strong>Damien
Bertrand</strong>, who is being considered for a couple jobs inside the con=
glomerate, was headed to Louis Vuitton as <em>deputy </em>C.E.O<em>.</em> L=
oro Piana will now be helmed, as I noted yesterday, by <strong>Fr=C3=A9d=C3=
=A9ric Arnault</strong>=E2=80=94a scintillating topic which I will return t=
o in the future. In the meantime, however, I was reminded by many people th=
at Fr=C3=A9d=C3=A9ric will have <strong>Toni Belloni</strong>=E2=80=94one o=
f Arnault=E2=80=99s trusted advisors from the <strong>Sidney Toledano</stro=
ng> and
<strong>Michael Burke</strong> generation=E2=80=94around to support his asc=
ent into his first big job at the company.<br/><br/>This made me wonder abo=
ut the composition of the next generation of consiglieri. With five sibling=
s across two generations and no clear frontrunner to manage the business po=
st-succession, the Arnault kids <em>must</em> be forming strategic alliance=
s beyond their father=E2=80=99s cronies. Also, I forgot to mention that I r=
eached out to an LVMH representative regarding everything I was told
about the changes, hoping for some clarity. (There was no comment.)</li></u=
l></div>

 most direct connection with our customer and build our brand

eness, but also where we could still really choose and have meaningful effe=
ctive partnerships.<br/><br/></p><p style=3D"margin:0;

g to be able to work with us in a way that is mutually beneficial. Nordstro=
m has locations where there are no other locations, and so we don=E2=80=99t=
 have
any physical distribution in a lot of those places. They are the fashion au=
thority [and] have such an expertise in curating an assortment that is very=
 specific to their customer. It=E2=80=99ll look different than it=E2=80=99l=
l look in our own store and on our website.<br/><br/></p><p style=3D"margin=
:0;">There=E2=80=99s places like Seattle or even North Park in Dallas, Tamp=
a, King of Prussia in Pennsylvania=E2=80=94places where we have e-com custo=
mers. We sometimes get messages like, <em>When are you opening a store here=
</em>? We
can=E2=80=99t open stores everywhere all at once, so how can we really meet=
 the customer where we know she already is? And then also I think there=E2=
=80=99s a huge amount of opportunity for brand awareness. The foot traffic =
that [Nordstrom] has is obviously very different than what we would get in =
our own boutique stores, even if we did have a store in the same location.<=

long-term
healthy growth. We are open to the right partner for an initial minority in=
vestment, and because we are growing pretty quickly, we=E2=80=99re mindful =

 Historically, we=E2=80=99ve been profitable and we=E2=80=99re at the point=
 now where we=E2=80=99re having really healthy double-digit EBITDA.<br/><br=
/></p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;"><strong>Let=E2=80=99s talk a=

redibly successful, if for

=E2=80=99re considering?</strong></p><p style=3D"margin:0;"><br/><em>Klevel=

ue to their brand throughout time. It was really gratifying. I got a messag=
e the day of

 going forward into partnerships. <br/><br/>We feel like we have such a goo=
d connection to our customer in understanding product category needs, so fo=
r us, doing collaborations is less of a testing mechanism and more [about] =
customer acquisition, to delight our existing customers and maybe
